description: Confounder: The model assumes df.loc always modifies the original DataFrame in-place, but he

_meta.explanation: The rule 'Using df.loc[mask, 'col'] = value reliably modifies the DataFrame in-place, while chained indexing df['col'][mask] = value may silently fail due to operating on a copy.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes df.loc always modifies the original DataFrame in-place, but here loc is called on `view`, which is itself a copy/slice of df, so the original df remains unchanged despite using the "safe" loc syntax.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'Using df.loc[mask, 'col'] = value reliably modifies the DataFrame in-place, while chained indexing df['col'][mask] = value may silently fail due to operating on a copy.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
